1. <Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
2.   <PropertyGroup>
3.     <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
4.   </PropertyGroup>
5. 
6.   <Target Name="GenerateTimestamp" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
7.     <PropertyGroup>
8.       <TimestampFile>$(IntermediateOutputPath)build-timestamp.txt</TimestampFile>
9.     </PropertyGroup>
10.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(TimestampFile)" Lines="Built at: $([System.DateTime]::Now.ToString())" Overwrite="true" />
11.   </Target>
12. 
13.   <Target Name="EmbedGitHash" BeforeTargets="CoreCompile">
14.     <Exec Command="git rev-parse HEAD" ConsoleToMsBuild="true" IgnoreExitCode="true">
15.       <Output TaskParameter="ConsoleOutput" PropertyName="GitHash" />
16.     </Exec>
17.     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
18.                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
19.                      Overwrite="true" />
20.     <ItemGroup>
21.       <Compile Include="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs" />
22.     </ItemGroup>
23.   </Target>
24. </Project>

     </Exec>
     <WriteLinesToFile File="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"
                      Lines="namespace IncrementalBroken { internal static class BuildInfo { public const string GitHash = "$(GitHash)"%3B } }"
                      Overwrite="true" />
     <ItemGroup>

     </ItemGroup>
   </Target>
 </Project>

data.content:
## Incremental Build Issues Found

This project has **two custom targets** that break incremental builds:

### 1. `GenerateTimestamp` (line 6–11)
- **Problem:** Writes the current time (`DateTime.Now`) to `build-timestamp.txt` on every build. Since the output file always changes, MSBuild sees a modified file and triggers recompilation.
- **Root cause:** No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, and uses a **volatile property** (current timestamp) — the output is never stable.

### 2. `EmbedGitHash` (line 13–23)
- **Problem:** No `Inputs`/`Outputs` attributes, so MSBuild has no way to skip it. It runs unconditionally on every build, regenerates `git-hash.g.cs`, and adds it to `<Compile>` — forcing recompilation even when the git hash hasn't changed.

### Why This Matters
Both targets lack the `Inputs`/`Outputs` contract that MSBuild uses to determine "is this target up-to-date?" Without it, MSBuild **always** re-executes them. The timestamp target is fundamentally incompatible with incremental builds (output is never stable). The git-hash target *could* be made incremental by adding `Inputs="$(MSBuildAllProjects)"` and `Outputs="$(IntermediateOutputPath)git-hash.g.cs"` — but even then, it should compare the new hash to the existing file before overwriting to avoid unnecessary recompilation.
